channel.title: Networking on Systology
channel.description: Recent content in Networking on Systology

item.title: Service Resilience
item.description: Design patterns for reliable microservice behavior under load; implementing strict request idempotency, non-blocking async I/O, robust circuit breakers, durable background queues, and observability.

item.title: Distributed Web Crawler
item.description: Design for a Google-scale web crawler focusing on Breadth-First Search (BFS), DNS resolution caching, robots.txt politeness, and handling duplicate or malicious domains.

item.title: URL Shortener & Pastebin
item.description: Design for a highly available, read-heavy service bridging short aliases to long URLs, focusing on Base62 encoding, Snowflake IDs, and collision avoidance.

item.title: Real-Time Collaborative WebApp
item.description: Design for a Google Docs or Figma style application using WebSockets, Operational Transformation (OT), or CRDTs to maintain consistent state among concurrent writers.

item.title: End-to-End Migration & Deduplication
item.description: System design for migrating large datasets with deduplication, integrity checks, resumability, and idempotence.

item.title: Global CDN Media Serving
item.description: CDN-backed media delivery architecture for low-latency, highly-available global media serving with background upload processing.

item.title: Mailprune
item.description: A highly effective, local-first email auditing and automated cleanup tool designed to definitively identify noisy senders and deliver actionable, strictly privacy-preserving recommendations.

item.title: Networking & Services
item.description: Operational guidance for resilient networked services; detailing robust API contracts, exponential retries with jitter, sliding-window rate limiting, strict timeout budgets, connection pooling, and mutual TLS.

item.title: Photohaul
item.description: A robust Java-based tool engineered for seamlessly organizing and migrating extensive photo collections; featuring rigorous deduplication, automatic metadata preservation, and resumable execution.
